fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity utah, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to utah, respiratory therapist compact utah, utah respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Utah DOPL — Respiratory Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Utah Division of Professional Licensing
P.O. Box 146741
Salt Lake City, UT 84114-6741

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Utah.

steps.title: Create or Log In to Your Account
steps.description: Access the Utah DOPL Online Application System at secure.utah.gov. Create a new UtahID account if you don't have one, or log in with your existing credentials.

steps.title: Start a New License Application
steps.description: Once logged in, navigate to the 'Apply for a New License' section. Select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' as the profession for which you are applying.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Fill out the comprehensive online application, providing all required personal, educational, examination, and professional history. Be sure to indicate if you are applying by endorsement from another state.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for all necessary supporting documents, such as official transcripts, verification of licensure from other states, and exam scores (if applicable), to be sent directly to DOPL.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable initial application fee of $85 through the Utah DOPL Online Application System. Your application will not be processed until the fee is paid.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through your account on the Utah DOPL Online Application System for updates or requests for additional information from the board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Utah. To work in Utah, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Utah
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Utah, you must submit an application through the Utah DOPL Online Application System.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Utah DOPL Online Application System, located at secure.utah.gov. Create a new account or log in with an existing UtahID account. Once logged in, navigate to 'Apply for a New License' to begin your application for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license.
